headline: Transcript: EU-Mercosur trade deal hangs in the balance
summary: Sonja Hutson talks to Andy Bounds and Amelia Pollard
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘EU-Mercosur trade deal hangs in the balance’

Sonja HutsonGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Tuesday, December 16th, and this is your FT News Briefing. Looks like Shell’s not so interested in BP. And a European trade deal decades in the making could be in jeopardy. Plus, hedge funds are diving into a new market — physical commodities. I’m Sonja Hutson, and here’s the news you need to start your day. [MUSIC PLAYING]Shell’s CEO shut down an internal proposal to buy the company’s rival BP this year. And after that, Shell’s head of mergers and acquisitions left the company. This is according to several people familiar with the situation. Its M&A team argued that BP’s falling share price and management turmoil opened up an opportunity. The thought was that a deal would help Shell solve its growth problems, but CEO Wael Sawan believed it would be challenging to combine two of Britain’s biggest companies. The i
